lines: Joanna Medicus knots a clean rag over a fever patient’s wrist and checks the pulse beneath it. Only then does she face you.
"Questions wait until the breathing settles. You may help the waiting by keeping your voice down."

choices.label: Ask about supplies and the donors
Ask how she protects the cots

lines: Joanna checks that the nearest patient remains asleep.

choices.label: Ask where the medicine came from
Offer to inspect the cellar stock

questUpdate.log: Joanna opens the charity trapdoor so the mixed medicine stock can be traced.

choices.label: Ask what the Chain asks in return

lines: Joanna lowers her voice until it does not carry past the cots.

choices.label: Ask why Compact care is not enough
Ask whether she keeps patient names
Say Salome noticed a shared fever pattern

lines: "Peter’s fever is falling while Rhoda’s lungs remain tight. Milcah has slept less than either of them."
"Those facts matter before faction or debt. Doctrine can wait outside."

choices.label: Let her return to the patients

lines: "Suppressant and gauze, with fever salts packed beneath them. The crates came at night with the donor marks scraped off."
"The vial lowered a child’s fever. If your office needs a fire, burn the card and leave me what works."

choices.label: Ask what she knows about the donors
Leave the stock in her hands

questUpdate.log: The charity prayer cards turn rejection into Choir teaching. Find who reads them after dark.

lines: "The words teach that a closed gate proves the body was refused, and that a wound admits everyone."
"I know the doctrine and have allowed no rite here. I did let a teacher read beside people everyone else left outside."
Joanna lifts the cot-side board and takes a short iron key from its hollow back.
"The trapdoor is under the charity canvas. Bring the cards back to me before anyone decides that ash is safer than medicine."
"The clinic screens before it treats. Screening needs forms and a stable line, with consent they can file."
"Fever does not wait for administrative weather."

choices.label: Leave her to the next dose

lines: "Chain medicine arrives with a debt number. Compact medicine arrives with a cohort number."
"Before dawn, I use people’s names and listen for breath."
"I burned the donor names off the crates. I will not give patient names to anyone who begins with custody."
"Bring me a supply plan and a consent plan. Then we can discuss records."
Joanna checks the cot board against Salome’s dose marks.
"Same evening chill in three patients, but only two used the new suppressant. That points to water or weather before medicine."
"Tell Noa the charity draw turned cloudy after the last pressure drop."

choices.label: Carry the observation to the water court

lines: "After dark, Salome teaches letters and reads whatever the patients ask for. Sometimes that includes the cards."
"A frightened person hearing dangerous words is not the same as a convert. I will watch for the difference."
